thread.title: Franklin Jr., FAMU storms past Bethune-Cookman to win fourth straight Florida Classic
thread.title_full: Franklin Jr., FAMU storms past Bethune-Cookman to win fourth straight Florida Classic

author: Ryan Kelly
title: Franklin Jr., FAMU storms past Bethune-Cookman to win fourth straight Florida Classic
text:
Franklin Jr., FAMU storms past Bethune-Cookman to win fourth straight Florida Classic
ORLANDO, Fla. (WCTV) - The Florida A&M Rattlers struck early, often and when it mattered most against their arch-rivals from Daytona Beach on Saturday, making just enough plays to outlast the Bethune-Cookman Wildcats 41-38 in FAMU’s fourth straight victory in the series.
The Rattlers would jump to a quick 7-0 lead following a 17 yard touchdown rush from Thad Franklin Jr., but B-CU would strike back quickly following a Maleek Huggins punt return TD to tie the game at seven.
FAMU would notch on a field goal to end the quarter and then open the scoring in the second as Daniel Richardson found Jamari Gassett for a 12 yard TD to give the designated road team a 17-7 advantage.
The rest of the quarter however belonged to the Wildcats, scoring 14 unanswered and taking a 21-17 advantage into halftime.
After a Bethune field goal extended the Rattler deficit to seven points, Franklin Jr. would find the end zone yet again from two yards out to tie the game at 24. A Cameron Gillis field goal gave FAMU a 27-24 lead into the fourth.
From there a wild fourth quarter ensued with the two bitter rivals trading scores starting with Franklin Jr.’s third and longest of the night from 70 yards after a missed B-CU field goal. Kelvin Dean would bring across what would eventually be the winning score, rushing to the end zone from 11 yards out.
James Colzie’s squad will host Alabama A&M on Friday at Bragg Memorial Stadium to finish the 2024 season.
